PixiJS V5 Tutorial (30) - The Game Loop
JavaScript
PixiJS V5 Tutorial
Canvas
w3HexSchool

Time to build the game loop. In short, it all comes down to app.ticker.add((delta) => {...});. In this part we wire up an HP bar that ticks down over time, hook the collision detection into the loop, and black out the screen once HP hits zero.
/en/javascript/2020/08/24/pixijs-v5-29-treasure-hunter-setup/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (29) - Building treasureHunter (the Treasure Hunter)
PixiJS V5 教學

Time to actually start building treasureHunter, our little Treasure Hunter game. In this part we'll slowly pull the pieces apart and put them back together to get a simple version up and running — loading the spritesheet, cutting out each sprite, and laying out the dungeon, monster, treasure, door, and player.
/en/javascript/2020/08/17/pixijs-v5-28-load-images-from-json/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (28) - Loading Images from JSON

Last chapter we learned how to crop images. This time we'll look at another way to load them — using a JSON file. With a JSON spritesheet you can slice up one image into multiple sprites, and if anything changes later you just tweak the JSON. This post walks through how it works in PixiJS V5.
/en/javascript/2020/08/10/pixijs-v5-27-crop-sprite-texture/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (27) - Cropping Images with Rectangle and Texture

Next up we're going to load images through JSON, but before that let's learn how to crop an image. In this post I'll show you how to pull a single piece out of a sprite sheet using PIXI.Rectangle, why your sprites get overwritten when you reuse the same texture, and how copying the Texture fixes it.
/en/javascript/2020/08/03/pixijs-v5-26-treasure-hunter/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (26) - Treasure Hunter: Building a Real Mini-Game

Time to put everything together. From here on we'll start combining the techniques from earlier in the series to build an actual little game called Treasure Hunter. This post covers where the game assets come from, which sprites we'll be using, and why loading images through a JSON file beats loading them one by one.
/en/javascript/2020/07/27/pixijs-v5-25-dust-particles/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (25) - Particle Effects the Easy Way with Dust
Time to get into particle effects. In this post I'll skip the native approach and jump straight to a plugin called Dust, which makes building explosions, flames, and star bursts way easier in PixiJS.
/en/javascript/2020/07/21/pixijs-v5-24-animatedsprite/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (24) - Frame-by-Frame Animation with AnimatedSprite

PixiJS is a great framework for building 2D animations. But how do you actually write an animation in Pixi? In this post we'll use AnimatedSprite to build a frame-by-frame animation, load a sequence of images, play it, and tweak the speed with dat.GUI.
/en/javascript/2020/07/15/pixijs-v5-23-dat-gui/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (23) - Tweaking Values Live with dat.GUI

In this post we actually wire up dat.GUI so you can tweak things like velocity and alpha live, without editing the code and refreshing every time. We adjust the previous example, map the GUI controls to the right spots, and finish with a quick look at the PixiJS Chrome devTools extension.
/en/javascript/2020/07/07/pixijs-v5-22-debug-datgui/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (22) - Debugging Pixi, and Meet dat.GUI

Debugging in Pixi is genuinely painful — there's no DOM to inspect in devTools, so tweaking parameters is a slog of edit, save, refresh, repeat. This post introduces dat.GUI, a tool that gives you a graphical panel to adjust your objects live. We'll get familiar with it here and actually wire it into Pixi in the next post.
/en/javascript/2020/07/01/pixijs-v5-21-collision-boundary-03/
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (21) - Collision Boundaries (3): Random Positions and Centering Text

This one picks up where the last post left off and fixes a few small things, so most of the code carries over from before — which means this post is a bit shorter. We'll randomize where the crit effect spawns using Math.random, and fix the text so it sits dead-center in the star shape.
